name: fparser

metadata.name: fparser
metadata.summary: Python implementation of a Fortran parser

documentation:
# News #
  * 31/01/2024 Version 0.1.4 released (resolves issues with WHERE statements and intrinsics).
  * 18/09/2023 Version 0.1.3 released (resolves issues with support for DO CONCURRENT).
  * 19/06/2023 Version 0.1.2 released (bug fix for handling of INCLUDE files).
  * 28/04/2023 Version 0.1.1 released (extends F2008 support with the optional
    "::" in the MODULE PROCEDURE statement).
  * 18/04/2023 Version 0.1.0 released and status changed from `alpha` to `beta`. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md) for more details.
  * 16/06/2022 Version 0.0.16 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 30/05/2022 Version 0.0.15 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 10/03/2022 Version 0.0.14 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 02/11/2021 Version 0.0.13 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 26/04/2021 Version 0.0.12 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 15/05/2020 Version 0.0.11 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 18/11/2019 Version 0.0.10 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 04/11/2019 Version 0.0.9 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 03/12/2018 Version 0.0.8 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 23/04/2018 Version 0.0.7 released. See the [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)
    for details.
  * 22/03/2017 Version 0.0.2 released. Documentation made available
    on [ReadTheDocs](http://fparser.readthedocs.io).
  * 16/01/2017 Parser code extracted from F2PY project to form the
    base of the fparser project.

# Introduction #

This project is based upon the Fortran (77..2003) parser originally
developed by Pearu Peterson for the F2PY project,
[www.f2py.com](http://www.f2py.com). It provides a parser for Fortran
source code implemented purely in Python with minimal dependencies.

# Obtaining #

fparser is available from the [Python Package Index](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/fparser).
It may also be cloned from [github](https://github.com/stfc/fparser). Alternatively, github provides
tarballs for each of the [releases](https://github.com/stfc/fparser/releases).

# Documentation #

The documentation for fparser is hosted on [ReadTheDocs](http://fparser.readthedocs.io).

# Code Metrics #

![fparser unit tests](https://github.com/stfc/fparser/workflows/fparser%20unit%20tests/badge.svg)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/stfc/fparser/branch/master/graph/badge.svg?token=x0abs66TST)](https://codecov.io/gh/stfc/fparser)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/fparser/badge/?version=stable)](http://fparser.readthedocs.io/en/stable/?badge=stable)

![Icicle coverage plot generated by CodeCov](https://codecov.io/gh/stfc/fparser/graphs/icicle.svg?token=x0abs66TST)

# See also #

[www.f2py.com](http://www.f2py.com).

modules.name: fparser.scripts.fparser2_bench
documentation:
Generates a large Fortran program in memory and then measures how long
it takes fparser2 to parse it. This is based on the benchmark suggested
by Ondřej Čertík via Ioannis Nikiteas.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.fparser2_bench.gen_sub
documentation:
Constructs a Fortran subroutine named g\<num>.

```
param num

the number of the subroutine (used to name it).

returns

Fortran subroutine.

rtype

str
```

functions.name: fparser.scripts.fparser2_bench.create_bench
documentation:
Creates the Fortran benchmark code.

```
param num_routines

the number of subroutines to create.

returns

benchmark Fortran code.

rtype

str
```

functions.name: fparser.scripts.fparser2_bench.runner
documentation:
Entry point for running the benchmark.

```
param num_routines

the number of subroutines to create in the                          Fortran benchmark.

raises ValueError

if num_routines < 1.
```

documentation:
Call the Fortran File reader for each filename in args and print
out its content.

```
param options

command line argument information from the options     parser

type options



param args

a list of Fortran filepaths

type args

list of str

raises NotImplementedError

if the task option is not set to     "show".
```

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_no_files
documentation: Test that the script deals with no files provided as expected.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_non_existant_file
documentation:
Test that the script reports an error when the file name that is
provided does not exist.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_no_file_multi
documentation:
Test that the script reports an error when the file name that is
provided does not exist and continues to parse any subsequent
file(s).

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_set_mode
documentation: Test that the script can change mode.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_syntax_error
documentation: Test that the script deals with code with an invalid syntax.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_internal_error
documentation:
Test that the script deals with an internal error as
expected. Provide the same file twice to check that the parser
continues to parse any additional files.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_output_task_show
documentation:
Test that the script outputs the code it has parsed with the
'task' option set to "show".

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_output_task_repr
documentation:
Test that the script outputs the repr representation of the code it
has parsed with the 'task' option set to "repr".

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_output_task_none
documentation:
Test that the script outputs nothing when the 'task' option is set
to "none".

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_multi_output
documentation:
Test that the script outputs the code it has parsed when there are
multiple files specified

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_runner_multi_output_except
documentation:
Test that if the script encounters a file with a syntax error then
it continues trying to parse subsequent files.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_main_output_task_default
documentation:
Test that the script main() function outputs the code it has parsed
by default and that no errors are logged.
fparser2.py script function main()

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_main_output_task_show
documentation:
Test that the script main() function outputs the code it has parsed
when --task=show.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_main_output_task_repr
documentation:
Test that the script main() function outputs the 'repr' of the code
it has parsed when --task=repr.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_main_output_task_none
documentation:
Test that the script main() function outputs nothing when
--task=none.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_main_output_task_invalid
documentation:
Test that the script main() function prints an error when an
invalid task option is provided.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_main_output_std_f2003
documentation:
Test that the script main() function parses using the Fortran2003
standard when --std=f2003. This test attempts to use submodules
which are not supported in Fortran2003 so should give a syntax
error.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_main_output_std_f2008
documentation:
Test that the script main() function parses using the Fortran2008
standard when --std=f2008. This test attempts to use submodules
which are supported in Fortran200 so should be parsed correctly.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_main_output_std_invalid
documentation:
Test that the script main() function raises an exception when an
invalid standard --std=invalid is provided.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_read_runner_nofiles
documentation: Test that the script deals with no files provided as expected.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_read_runner_file
documentation: Test that the script deals with one file as expected.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_read_runner_files
documentation: Test that the script deals with multiple files as expected.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_read_runner_no_show
documentation:
Test that the script raises an exception if the task argument !=
"show" as that is the only option currently supported.

functions.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.test_read_main
documentation: Test that the main function works as expected.

classes.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.DummyArgs
documentation: dummy object pretending to be the argument options
fparser2.py script function runner()
Create a dummy class (DummyArgs) with the required attribute to pass
into runner() as an argument options class in subsequent tests

classes.name: fparser.scripts.tests.test_scripts.DummyReadArgs
documentation:
dummy object pretending to be the argument options for the read
module in scripts.

:param str task: the value to set the internal task
variable. Defaults to "show".
read.py script function runner()
Create a dummy class (DummyReadArgs) with the required attribute to pass
into runner() as an argument options class in subsequent tests

classes.name: fparser.two.pattern_tools.Pattern

methods.name: fparser.two.pattern_tools.Pattern.rsplit
documentation:
Return (\<lhs>, \<pattern_match>, \<rhs>) where:

```
string = lhs + pattern_match + rhs
```

and rhs does not contain pattern_match.
If no pattern_match is found in string, return None.

methods.name: fparser.two.pattern_tools.Pattern.lsplit
documentation:
Return (\<lhs>, \<pattern_match>, \<rhs>) where:

```
string = lhs + pattern_match + rhs
```

and rhs does not contain pattern_match.
If no pattern_match is found in string, return None.

```
p1 | p2    -> <p1> | <p2>
p1 + p2    -> <p1> <p2>
p1 & p2    -> <p1><p2>
~p1        -> [ <p1> ]
~~p1       -> [ <p1> ]...
~~~p1      -> <p1> [ <p1> ]...
~~~~p1     -> ~~~p1
abs(p1)    -> whole string match of <p1>
p1.named(name) -> match of <p1> has name
p1.match(string) -> return string match with <p1>
p1.flags(<re.I,..>)
p1.rsplit(..) -> split a string from the rightmost p1 occurrence
p1.lsplit(..) -> split a string from the leftmost p1 occurrence
```

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2008.test_loop_control_r818
documentation:
Test Fortran 2008 rule R818

loop-control is [ , ] do-variable = scalar-int-expr , scalar-int-expr
                   [ , scalar-int-expr ]
                or [ , ] WHILE ( scalar-logical-expr )
                or [ , ] CONCURRENT forall-header

Extends the Fortran2003 rule R830 with the additional CONCURRENT clause.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2008.test_block_label_do_construct_r814_1
documentation:
Test Fortran 2008 rule R814_1

    block-do-construct is do-stmt
                          do-block
                          end-do

The implementation in fparser2 actually implements the case when
do-stmt is a label-do-stmt (hence the name R814_1). R814_2 implements
the case when do-stmt is a nonlabel-do-stmt.

The only difference to F2003 rule R835 is that we force this rule to
use the F2008 version of label-do-stmt

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2008.test_submodule_r1116
documentation:
Test Fortran 2008 rule R1116 and its constraints C1112 and C1114.

submodule is submodule-stmt
             [ specification-part ]
             [ module-subprogram-part ]
             end-submodule-stmt

C1112 A submodule specification-part shall not contain a
format-stmt, entry-stmt, or stmt-function-stmt.

C1114 If a submodule-name appears in the end-submodule-stmt, it
shall be identical to the one in the submodule-stmt.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2008.test_open_stmt_r904
documentation:
Test Fortran 2008 rule R904

   open-stmt is OPEN ( connect-spec-list )

   R905 connect-spec is [ UNIT = ] file-unit-number
                    or ACCESS = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or ACTION = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or ASYNCHRONOUS = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or BLANK = scalar-default-char-expr
                    [ or CONVERT = scalar-default-char-expr ]
                    or DECIMAL = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or DELIM = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or ENCODING = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or ERR = label
                    or FILE = file-name-expr
                    or FORM = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or IOMSG = iomsg-variable
                    or IOSTAT = scalar-int-variable
                    or NEWUNIT = scalar-int-variable
                    or PAD = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or POSITION = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or RECL = scalar-int-expr
                    or ROUND = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or SIGN = scalar-default-char-expr
                    or STATUS = scalar-default-char-expr

Note that the CONVERT argument is non-standard but supported by several major
compilers.

R906 file-name-expr is scalar-default-char-expr
R907 iomsg-variable is scalar-default-char-variable

C903 No specifier shall appear more than once in a given connect-spec-list.
C904 (R904) If the NEWUNIT= specifier does not appear, a file-unit-number
     shall be specified; if the optional characters UNIT= are omitted, the
     file-unit-number shall be the first item in the connect-spec-list.
C905 (R904) The label used in the ERR= specifier shall be the statement label
     of a branch target statement that appears in the same inclusive scope as
     the OPEN statement.
C906 (R904) If a NEWUNIT= specifier appears, a file-unit-number shall not
     appear.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2008.test_parent_identifier_r1118
documentation:
Test Fortran 2008 rule R1118 (C1113).

parent-identifier is ancestor-module-name [ : parent-submodule-name ]

C1113 The ancestor-module-name shall be the name of a nonintrinsic
module; the parent-submodule name shall be the name of a
descendant of that module.

It is not possible to test C1113 with fparser as the names may be
in different files.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2008.test_action_stmt_r214
documentation:
Test Fortran 2008 rule R214

action-stmt is allocate-stmt
                or assignment-stmt
                or backspace-stmt
                or call-stmt
                or close-stmt
                or continue-stmt
                or cycle-stmt
                or deallocate-stmt
                or end-function-stmt
                or end-mp-subprogram-stmt
                or end-program-stmt
                or end-subroutine-stmt
                or endfile-stmt
                or error-stop-stmt
                or exit-stmt
                or flush-stmt
                or forall-stmt
                or goto-stmt
                or if-stmt
                or inquire-stmt
                or lock-stmt
                or nullify-stmt
                or open-stmt
                or pointer-assignment-stmt
                or print-stmt
                or read-stmt
                or return-stmt
                or rewind-stmt
                or stop-stmt
                or sync-all-stmt
                or sync-images-stmt
                or sync-memory-stmt
                or unlock-stmt
                or wait-stmt
                or where-stmt
                or write-stmt
                or arithmetic-if-stmt
                or computed-goto-stmt

Associated constraints are:

"C201 (R208) An execution-part shall not contain an end-function-stmt,
      end-mp-subprogram-stmt, end-program-stmt, or end-subroutine-stmt."

TODO: Implement and test lock-stmt, sync-all-stmt, sync-images-stmt,
      sync-memory-stmt, unlock-stmt (#321)

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_default_char_expr_r726.test_c707
documentation:
Check that invalid default literal constants do not match. Note,
there are many other cases that are not currently checked in
fparser.

In theory -1 should become a signed_int_literal_constant and then
fail to match. Similarly -1.0 should become a
Signed_Real_Literal_Constant and then fail to match. However the
"-" is treated as a unary expression so this never happens.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_hollerith_item
documentation:
Test Fortran 2003 : This file tests the support for a Hollerith
string. Hollerith strings take the form `nHx`, where `n` is an integer
and `x` is a sequence of characters of length `n`.

Note, the Hollerith format was deprecated in Fortran77 and removed in
Fortran95. However, Fortran compilers still support it.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.test_c99preprocessor
documentation:
Test C99 Preprocessor directives: This file tests the preprocessor
directives defined in the C99 standard. Whilst this is not part of any
Fortran standard, most compilers support preprocessing of Fortran files
and support the majority of directives from the C-Standard. Since this
is actively used in user codes there are cases where users might like to
keep the directives in the Fortran parse tree and output it again.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_numeric_expr_r728.test_c709
documentation:
Check that invalid literal constants do not match. Note, there are
many other cases that are not currently checked in fparser.

In theory -1.0 should become a Signed_Real_Literal_Constant and
then fail to match. However the "-" is treated as a unary
expression so this never happens.

documentation:
Test that fparser2 supports the various keywords specified in R905
for the connect-spec that expect a scalar, character value. Note
that CONVERT is not actually part of the standard but is supported
by at least Gnu, Intel and Cray.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_main_program_r1101
documentation:
Test Fortran 2003 rule R1101 : This file tests the support for a
main program with a program statement. It does not test the case where
there is no program statement. That situation is covered by the
Main_Program0 class. Tests for Main_Program0 are currently in
test_fortran2003.py.

As this rule is about ordering of other rules we only need to test the
different combinations. We don't need to test the content of
individual rules (e.g we do not need to worry about "end", "end
program", "end program a" as these should be covered by the tests for
End_Program_Unit

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_kind
documentation:
Test that a kind selector with 'kind=' present can be parsed
successfully.
match() 'kind=' is present

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_kind_case
documentation:
Test that a kind selector with 'kind=' present with mixed case can
be parsed successfully.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_kind_spaces
documentation:
Test that a kind selector with 'kind=' present and spaces can be
parsed successfully.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_nokind
documentation:
Test that a kind selector with 'kind=' not present can be parsed
successfully. Note, the parser automatically adds-in kind for the
output.
match() 'kind=' is not present

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_nokind_spaces
documentation:
Test that a kind selector with 'kind=' not present and spaces can
be parsed successfully. Note, the parser automatically adds-in
kind for the output.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_nokind_short
documentation:
Test that a kind selector with 'kind=' not present and a name less
than 6 characters can be parsed successfully. This is important as
there is a pattern match for 'kind=' so we need to test we don't
accidentally access outside of the string. Note, the parser
automatically adds-in kind for the output.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_nokind_kind_function
documentation:
Test that a kind selector with 'kind=' not present and a kind
function can be parsed successfully. This is a test as there is a
risk that the pattern match will think the kind function is the
start of 'kind='. Note, the parser automatically adds-in kind for
the output.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_size_extension
documentation:
Test that a kind selector with the '*number' format e.g. integer*4
can be parsed successfully. This is an extension to the standard
but is well used and is supported in mainstream compilers so is
also supported here. Note, we can't use a reader as it will not
read '*2' so we pass the string directly.
match() *n extension

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_size_extension_spaces
documentation:
Test that a kind selector with the '*number' format e.g. integer*4
can be parsed successfully if there are spaces between the * and
the number. This is an extension to the standard but is well used
and is supported in mainstream compilers so is also supported
here. Note, we can't use a reader as it will not read '*2' so we
pass the string directly.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_error_left_bracket
documentation: Test that None is returned if the left bracket is missing.
match() Syntax errors

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_error_right_bracket
documentation: Test that None is returned if the right bracket is missing.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_error_kind_name
documentation: Test that None is returned if the name 'kind' is incorrect.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_error_kind_equals
documentation: Test that None is returned if the '=' after 'kind' is not there.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_error_star_only
documentation:
Test that an InternalError is raised if there are less than two
characters in the string as valid input must have at least two.
match() Internal errors

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_error_empty
documentation: Test that an InternalError is raised if the input is empty.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_error_none
documentation: Test that an InternalError is raised if the input is None.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_tostr_error_nitems
documentation:
Test that the appropriate exception is raised if the number of
elements in the self.items list is >3 or <2.
tostr() Errors

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_kindselector_r404.test_to_be_moved
documentation:
Show that an example with a space before a kind specification
fails. This is not the fault of this class but I'm including it
here for the moment until the reason for the error is fixed.
misc

classes.name: fparser.two.utils.NoMatchError

An exception indicating that a particular rule implemented by a
class does not match the provided string. It does not necessary
mean there is an error as another rule may match. This exception
is used internally so should never be visible externally.

documentation:
Call the method, if other is able to be used within it.

```
param object other

The other object to compare with

object

LambdaType

NotImplemented, when the comparison for the given type
combination can't be performed.

pylint: disable=too-few-public-methods

Mixin class to provide rich comparison operators.

This mixin provides a set of rich comparison operators. Each class using
this mixin has to provide a _cmpkey() method that returns a key of objects
that can be compared.

See also http://python3porting.com/preparing.html#richcomparisons
BASE CLASSES

classes.name: fparser.two.utils.DynamicImport

methods.name: fparser.two.utils.DynamicImport.import_now
documentation: Execute the Import of Fortran2003 dependencies.

This class imports a set of fparser.two dependencies that can not
be imported during the Python Import time because they have a circular
dependency with this file.

They are imported once when the Fortran2003 is already processed by
calling the import_now() method.

The alternative is to have the equivalent top-level imports in the
Base.__new__ method, but this method is in the parser critical path and
is best to keep expensive operations out of it.

classes.name: fparser.two.utils.BinaryOpBase

methods.name: fparser.two.utils.BinaryOpBase.match
documentation:
Matches the binary-op-base rule.

If the operator defined by argument 'op_pattern' is found in
the string provided in argument 'string' then the text to the
left-hand-side of the operator is matched with the class rule
provided in the 'lhs_cls' argument and the text to the
right-hand-side of the operator is matched with the class rule
provided in the 'rhs_cls' argument.

If the optional 'right' argument is set to true (the default)
then, in the case where the pattern matches multiple times in
the input string, the right-most match will be chosen. If the
'right' argument is set to false then the left-most match will
be chosen.

if a pattern is provided to the optional 'exclude_op_pattern'
argument then there will be no match if the pattern matched by
the 'op_pattern' argument also matches this pattern. The
default (None) does nothing.

```
param lhs_cls

an fparser2 object representing the rule that             should be matched to the lhs text.

type lhs_cls

subclass of 

param op_pattern

the pattern to match.

type op_pattern

 or             

param rhs_cls

an fparser2 object representing the rule that             should be matched to the rhs text.

type rhs_cls

subclass of 

param str string

the string to match with the pattern and             lhs and rhs rules.

param bool right

in the case where there are multiple             matches to the pattern in the string this optional             argument specifies whether the righmost pattern match             should be chosen (True, the default) or whether the             leftmost pattern should be chosen (False).

param exclude_op_pattern

optional argument which specifies a             particular subpattern to exclude from the match. Defaults             to None which means there is no subpattern.

type exclude_op_pattern



returns

a tuple containing the matched lhs, the operator and             the matched rhs of the input string or None if there is             no match.

rtype

(, str,             ) or NoneType
```

methods.name: fparser.two.utils.BinaryOpBase.tostr
documentation:
Return the string representation of this object. Uses join() which
is efficient and can make a big performance difference for
complex expressions.

```
returns

the string representation of this object.

rtype

str
```

```
binary-op-base is lhs op rhs
```

Splits the input text into text to the left of the matched
operator and text to the right of the matched operator and tries
to match the lhs text with the supplied lhs class rule and the rhs
text with the supplied rhs class rule.

classes.name: fparser.two.utils.KeywordValueBase

methods.name: fparser.two.utils.KeywordValueBase.match
documentation:
Attempts to match the supplied `string` with `lhs_cls` = `rhs_cls`.
If `lhs_cls` is a str then it is compared with the content to the
left of the first '=' character in `string`. If that content is a
valid Fortran name but does *not* match `lhs_cls` then the match
fails, irrespective of the setting of `require_lhs`.

```
param lhs_cls

list, tuple or single value of classes to attempt to                         match LHS against (in order), or string containing                         keyword to match.

type lhs_cls

names of classes deriving from  or str

param rhs_cls

name of class to match RHS against.

type rhs_cls

name of a class deriving from 

param str string

text to be matched.

param bool require_lhs

whether the expression to be matched must                                  contain a LHS that is assigned to.

param bool upper_lhs

whether or not to convert the LHS of the                                matched expression to upper case.

return

instances of the classes representing quantities on the LHS                  and RHS (LHS is optional) or None if no match is found.

rtype

2-tuple of objects or NoneType
```

```
keyword-value-base is [ lhs = ] rhs
```

where:

```
R215 keyword is name.
```

methods.name: fparser.two.utils.BracketBase.match
documentation:
A generic match method for all types of bracketed
expressions.

```
param str brackets

the format of the left and right brackets         provided as a string, for example '()'

param cls

the class to match the content within the brackets         :type cls: subclass of 

param str string

the content to match

param bool require_cls

whether the class and associated         content is mandatory (True) or optional (False). The default         is True.

return

None if there is no match, otherwise a tuple with the         first and third entries being strings containing the left and         right brackets respectively and the second entry being either         None or an instance of the class provided as the second         argument (cls).

rtype

'NoneType', ( , , ) or ( ,         ,  )
```

This class is able to cope with nested brackets as long as they
are correctly nested. Brackets in strings are ignored.

The 'something' can be specified as being optional.

documentation:
---
param bool require_rhs: whether the rhs (the part within parentheses) must be present.
param bool upper_lhs: whether or not to convert the lhs to uppercase before attempting
  the match.
param lhs_cls: the class to match with the lhs.
param rhs_cls: the class to match with the rhs.
param str string: the string to attempt to match.
returns: a tuple containing the lhs and rhs matches or None if there is no match.
rtype: Optional\[Tuple\[{py:class}`fparser.two.utils.Base`, Optional\[{py:class}`fparser.two.utils.Base`\]\]\]
type lhs_cls: str | class
type rhs_cls: str | class
---

```
call-base is lhs ( [ rhs ] )
```

classes.name: fparser.two.utils.STRINGBase

methods.name: fparser.two.utils.STRINGBase.match
documentation:
Matches an input string with a specified pattern. Casts the string
to upper case before performing a match and, if there is a
match, returns the string in upper case.

The pattern can be a regular expression, a string, a list or a
tuple. If the input pattern is a regular expression or a
string, a direct equivalence is performed. If the input pattern is a
list or a tuple, then all of the contents of the list
or tuple are searched for a match (by recursing). The list or tuple may
contain regular expressions, strings, lists or tuples. This
functionality can be used to recurse down a tree of lists and
or tuples until regular expressions or strings are found (at
the leaves of the tree) on which to match. The patterns used
to match in fparser can be found in patterns_tools.py. These
make use of the pattern class, whose match method behaves like
a regular expression. For example:

from fparser.two import pattern_tools
pattern = pattern_tools.intrinsic_type_name
result = STRINGBase.match(pattern, "logical")

```
param pattern

the pattern to match

type pattern

, ,  or an  expression

param str string

the string to match with the pattern

return

None if there is no match, or a tuple containing the         matched string in upper case.

rtype

 or (  )
```

STRINGBase matches an upper case version of the input string with
another a pattern (typically taken from pattern_tools.py) and
returns the string in upper case if there is a match.

classes.name: fparser.two.utils.WORDClsBase

methods.name: fparser.two.utils.WORDClsBase.match
documentation:
Checks whether the content in string matches the expected
WORDClsBase format with 'keyword' providing the keyword, 'cls'
providing the following text, 'colons' specifying whether an
optional double colon is allowed as a separator between the keyword
and cls and 'require_cls' specifying whether cls must have
content or not.

Note, if the optional double colon is allowed and exists in the string
then 1) cls must also have content i.e. it implies
`require_cls=True` and 2) white space is not required between
the keyword and the double colon and the double colon and cls.

The simplest form of keyword pattern is a string. However this
method can also match more complex patterns as specified by
the Pattern class in pattern_tools.py. As patterns can be
built from combinations of other patterns (again see
pattern_tool.py) this method also supports a hierarchy of
lists and/or tuples of patterns.

```
param keyword

the pattern of the WORD to match. This can be             a Pattern, string, list or tuple, with a list or tuple             containing one or more Pattern, string, list or tuple.

type keyword

,             str, tuple of str/Pattern/tuple/list or list of             str/Pattern/tuple/list

param cls

the class to match.

type cls

a subclass of 

param str string

Text that we are trying to match.

param bool colons

whether a double colon is allowed as an optional             separator between between WORD and cls.

param bool require_cls

whether content for cls is required             or not.

returns

None if there is no match or, if there is a match, a             2-tuple containing a string matching the 'WORD' and an             instance of 'cls' (or None if an instance of cls is not             required and not provided).

rtype

Optional[Tupe[Str, Optional[Cls]]]
```

```

methods.name: fparser.two.utils.WORDClsBase.tostr_a
documentation:

```

rtype

str
```

Base class to support situations where there is a keyword which is
optionally followed by further text, potentially separated by
a double colon. For example:

```
'program fred', or 'import :: a,b'

WORD-cls is WORD [ [ :: ] cls ]
```

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_logical_expr_r724.test_c705
documentation:
Check that invalid literal constants do not match. Note, there are
many other cases that are not currently checked in fparser.

In theory -1 should become a signed_int_literal_constant and then
fail to match. Similarly -1.0 should become a
Signed_Real_Literal_Constant and then fail to match. However the
"-" is treated as a unary expression so this never happens.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_include_statement
documentation:

include statement. Whilst include is not part of the standard Fortran
rules (the include should include code as the code is being parsed)
there are cases where users might like to keep the include statement
in the Fortran parse tree and output it again.

correctly. Input separately as a string and as a reader object

documentation:
Check that spaces are allowed before and after an include keyword
as well as after the file string.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_include_statement.test_include_filename_error
documentation:
Check that we raise an InternalError if a return from
Include_Filename is None or an empty string. This should never
happen as any matching errors would cause this class to raise an
exception.

long, the name matches an existing intrinsic operator (e.g. .gt.),

(e.g. .false.).

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_c1002.test_data_edit_descriptor_error
documentation:
Check that None is returned if the descriptor following a P edit
descriptor is not of the expected type. What is expected is a
Format_Item instance containing a Data_Edit_Descriptor as its
second item. This test checks that we return None if the second
item is not a Data_Edit_Descriptor.

We do this by trying to match with a format-item-list as this is
the only other thing that returns a Format_Item instance. However,
it does not contain a Data_Edit_Descriptor as its second item so
it triggers the appropriate line of code.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_c1002.test_internal_errors1
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the length of the Items
list is not 2 as the str() method assumes that it is.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_c1002.test_internal_error2
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if entry 0 of items is empty
or None as the str() method assumes that it has content.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_c1002.test_internal_error3
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if entry 1 of items is empty
or None as the str() method assumes that it has content.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_empty_input
documentation:
Test that empty input or input only containing white space can be
parsed succesfully
Test no content or just white space. This is not officially a
Fortran rule but fortran compilers tend to accept empty content so
we follow their lead.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_only_comments
documentation:
Test that a file containing only comments can be parsed
successfully

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_single
documentation: Test that a single program_unit can be parsed successfully.
Test single program units

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_single_with_end_name
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit can be parsed successfully when it
has a name specified on the end clause.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_single2
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit with 5 or more spaces at the start
of the line reports an error on the correct (first) line

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_single3
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit with 5 or more spaces at the start
of the line reports an error on the correct (second) line

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_single_error1
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit with an error in the initial
statement raises an appropriate exception

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_single_error2
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit with an error in the final
statement raises an appropriate exception

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_multiple
documentation: Test that multiple program_units can be parsed successfully.
Test multiple program units

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_multiple_error1
documentation:
Test that multiple program_units with the same name raise an
exception

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_multiple_error2
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit with an error raises an appropriate
exception

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_multiple_error3
documentation:
Test that multiple program_units with an error raises an
appropriate exception

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_missing_prog
documentation:
Test that a main program program_unit without a program declaration
can be parsed successfully. This should not really be a test here,
but this case is currently treated separately by the match method
in Program.
Test a program unit with a missing program statement

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_missing_prog_multi
documentation:
Test that a main program program_unit without a program declaration
can be parsed successfully when it is not the first program_unit.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_one_main1
documentation: Test that multiple main programs raise an exception.
A program should contain only only one main program

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_comment0
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit without comments can be parsed
successfully with comment processing switched on.
Check comments are supported at this level

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_comment1
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit can be parsed successfully with
comments being ignored.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_comment2
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit can be parsed successfully with
comments being included.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_comment3
documentation:
Test that multiple program_units can be parsed successfully with
comments being ignored.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_comment4
documentation:
Test that multiple program_units can be parsed successfully with
comments being included.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_include0
documentation:
Test that a single program_unit with includes can be parsed
succesfully.
Check includes are supported at this level

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_include1
documentation:
Test that multiple program_units with includes can be parsed
successfully.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_program_r201.test_mix
documentation:
Test that multiple program_units can be parsed successfully with a
mix of includes and comments.
Check a mix of includes and comments are supported at this level

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list
documentation:
Test Fortran 2003 : This file tests the support for a format item
list. List classes are usually generated, and therefore are not
directly tested, but in this case we need to make a specific list
class due to the complexity of supporting Hollerith format items.

Note, the hollerith format was deprecated in Fortran77 and removed in
Fortran95. However, Fortran compilers still support it.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list.test_non_hollerith
documentation:
Check that some basic format specifications are parsed
correctly. Individual specifications are tested by the associated
classes.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list.test_mixed_hollerith_1
documentation:
Check that a hollerith item is parsed correctly. We do this as a
hollerith item can contain commas so we can't use a simple
format-item-list class to split items.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list.test_mixed_hollerith_2
documentation:
Check that a hollerith item is parsed correctly. We do this as a
hollerith item can contain commas so we can't use a simple
format-item-list class to split items.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list.test_hollerith_only
documentation:
Check that a hollerith item is parsed correctly. We do this as a
hollerith item can contain commas so we can't use a simple
format-item-list class to split items.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list.test_hollerith_only_spaces
documentation:
Check that a hollerith item is parsed correctly in the presence of
additional spaces. We do this as a hollerith item must have the
correct number of characters so any spaces that are not required
must be removed.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list.test_hollerith_omitted_comma_before
documentation:
Check that a hollerith item is parsed correctly when preceeded by a
slash or colon without a separating comma.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list.test_hollerith_omitted_comma_after
documentation:
Check that a hollerith item is parsed correctly when followed by a
slash or colon without a separating comma.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list.test_hollerith_trailing_space
documentation:
Check that a hollerith item is parsed correctly at the end of a list
when it contains a trailing space.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_item_list.test_errors
documentation:
test some list errors. Individual item errors will be picked up by
the subclasses.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_data_edit_desc_c1002.test_wd
documentation:
Check that valid w.d format specifications are parsed
correctly. Also include an example with spaces and multiple
digits with spaces.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_data_edit_desc_c1002.test_wde
documentation:
Check that valid w.dEe format specifications are parsed
correctly. Also include an example with spaces and multiple
digits with spaces.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_data_edit_desc_c1002.test_errors
documentation:
Check that format specifications not conforming to the C1002 1st
clause, raise an NoMatchError exception.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_data_edit_desc_c1002.test_internal_error1
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the length of the Items
list is not 4 as the str() method assumes that it is.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_data_edit_desc_c1002.test_internal_error2
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the descriptor name
(entry 0 of items) is empty or None as the str() method assumes
that it is a string with content.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_data_edit_desc_c1002.test_internal_error3
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the w value
(entry 1 of items) is empty or None as the str() method assumes
that it is a string with content.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_data_edit_desc_c1002.test_internal_error4
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the m value (entry 2 of
items) is empty or None as the str() method assumes that it is a
string with content.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_data_edit_desc_c1002.test_internal_error5
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the e value (entry 3 of
items) has content when the descriptor is F or D as it should be
None.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_data_edit_desc_c1002.test_internal_error6
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the descriptor name
has an unexpected value.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.use_f2003
documentation:
A pytest fixture that just sets things up so that the f2003_create
fixture is used automatically for every test in this file.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use
documentation:
Check that a basic use is parsed correctly. Input separately as a
string and as a reader object
match() use ...
match() 'use x'. Use both string and reader input here, but from
here on we will just use string input as that is what is passed to
the match() method

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_colons
documentation: Check that a basic use with '::' is parsed correctly.
match() 'use :: x'

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_nature
documentation: Check that a use with a 'nature' specification is parsed correctly.
match() 'use, nature :: x'

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_rename
documentation: Check that a use with a rename clause is parsed correctly.
match() 'use x, rename'

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_operator_rename
documentation: Check that a use with a rename clause for an operator is parsed correctly.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_only
documentation:
Check that a use statement is parsed correctly when there is an
only clause. Test both with and without a scoping region.
match() 'use x, only: y'

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_only_empty
documentation:
Check that a use statement is parsed correctly when there is an
only clause without any content. Test both with and without a scoping region.
match() 'use x, only:'

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_only_plus_rename
documentation:
Check that a use statement with an only clause and some variable
renaming is parsed correctly.
match() 'use x, only: b => c'

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_only_operator
documentation: Check that a 'use, only' that imports an operator is parsed correctly.
match() 'use x, only: operator(-)'

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_only_renamed_operator
documentation:
Check that a 'use, only' that imports and locally renames an operator is
correctly parsed. We test both for an extended intrinsic operator and a
defined operator.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_spaces_1
documentation:
Check that a use statement with spaces works correctly with
renaming.
match() '  use  ,  nature  ::  x  ,  name=>new_name'

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_spaces_2
documentation:
Check that a use statement with spaces works correctly with an only
clause.
match() '  use  ,  nature  ::  x  ,  only  :  name'

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_mixed_case
documentation:
Check that a use statement with mixed case keywords ('use' and
'only') works as expected.
match() mixed case

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_syntaxerror
documentation: Test that NoMatchError is raised for various syntax errors.
match() Syntax errors

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_internal_error1
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the length of the Items
list is not 5 as the str() method assumes that it is.
match() Internal errors

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_internal_error2
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the module name (entry 2
of Items) is empty or None as the str() method assumes that it is
a string with content.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_internal_error3
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if entry 3 of Items is
'None' as the str() method assumes it is a (potentially empty)
string.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_usestmt_r1109.test_use_internal_error_only_list
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if an Only_List contains an
unexpected entry.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_executable_construct_r213
documentation:
Test Fortran 2003 rule R213 : This file tests support for the
Exectuable_Construct class.

As this class just uses Base to match with a choice of subclasses we
test that an instance of each subclass can be succesfully
parsed. Detailed checking of the subclass rules are performed by the
subclass tests.

tests. We just check a few here to make sure the correct

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_recognised
documentation: Test that an intrinsic is picked up when used in a program.
class program

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_error
documentation:
Test that Program raises the expected exception when there is an
intrinsic syntax error.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_name_generic
documentation: Test that class Intrinsic_Name correctly matches a generic name.
class intrinsic_name

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_name_specific
documentation: Test that class Intrinsic_Name correctly matches a specific name.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_name_invalid
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Name raises the expected exception if an
invalid intrinsic name is provided.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_name_case_insensitive
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Name is a case insensitive match which
returns the name in upper case.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_reference_generic
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference correctly matches a
generic intrinsic with a valid number of arguments. We test both
with and without the existance of a symbol table.
class intrinsic_function_reference

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_reference
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference correctly matches a
specific intrinsic with a valid number of arguments.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_nomatch
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference raises the expected
exception if there is no match.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_reference_multi_args
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference correctly matches a
generic intrinsic which accepts more than one argument (two in
this case).

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_reference_zero_args
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference correctly matches a
generic intrinsic which accepts zero arguments.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_reference_range_args
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference correctly matches a
generic intrinsic which accepts a range of number of arguments.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_reference_unlimited_args
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference correctly matches a
generic intrinsic which accepts an unlimitednumber of arguments.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_reference_error1
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference raises the expected
exception when the valid min and max args are equal (2 in this case)
and the wrong number of arguments is supplied.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_reference_error2
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference raises the expected
exception when the valid min args is less than the valid max args
and the wrong number of arguments is supplied.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_function_reference_error3
documentation:
Test that class Intrinsic_Function_Reference raises the expected
exception when the number of arguments is unlimited.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_intrinsic_inside_intrinsic
documentation:
Test that when an intrinsic is within another instrinsic then both
are recognised as intrinsics.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_locally_shadowed_intrinsic
documentation:
Check that a locally-defined symbol that shadows (overwrites) a
Fortran intrinsic is correctly identified.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_shadowed_intrinsic_named_import
documentation:
Check that an imported symbol that shadows (overwrites) a
Fortran intrinsic is correctly identified.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_shadowed_intrinsic_import
documentation:
Check that an imported symbol that shadows (overwrites) a Fortran
intrinsic is not identified as an intrinsic if it has the wrong
number of 'arguments'.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_intrinsics.test_shadowed_intrinsic_error
documentation:
Check that an imported symbol that shadows (overwrites) a
Fortran intrinsic is not identified as an intrinsic if it has the wrong
types of argument. At the moment we are unable to check the types of
arguments (TODO #201) and so this test x-fails.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_char_expr_r725.test_c706
documentation:
Check that invalid literal constants do not match. Note, there are
many other cases that are not currently checked in fparser.

In theory -1 should become a signed_int_literal_constant and then
fail to match. Similarly -1.0 should become a
Signed_Real_Literal_Constant and then fail to match. However the
"-" is treated as a unary expression so this never happens.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_include_filename
documentation:
Test Fortran Include file name: This file tests the filename in an
include statement. Whilst include is not part of the standard Fortran
rules (a compiler should parse any code from an include as the code is
being parsed) there are cases where users might like to keep the
include statement in the Fortran parse tree and output it again.

The test for a valid file name is very lightweight as it is unclear
what would be invalid. The only things considered invalid are white
space at the beginning and/or end of the filename, or an empty string.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_include_filename.test_invalid_include_filename
documentation:
As the file name is not part of the Fortran standard, we apply very
loose rules to what is valid. The only things we don't accept are
an empty string or white space at the start or end of the string.

Check that valid control edit descriptors are parsed correctly when

and '$'. '$' is dealt with in separate tests as it is an
extension. We test with and without spaces.

they are passed onto subclasses. In this case we just test a
single example for each subclass to see if valid values are passed
on, as the subclass tests check all the options.

documentation:
Check that valid $ format specifications are parsed correctly if
the dollar-descriptor extension is specified. Also include an
example with spaces.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_control_edit_descriptor_r1011.test_dollar_invalid
documentation:
Check that valid '$' format specifications raise a NoMatchError if
the 'dollar-format' extension is not in the EXTENSIONS list.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_control_edit_descriptor_r1011.test_invalid_format_errors
documentation:
Check that invalid format for the match method raises a

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_control_edit_descriptor_r1011.test_internal_error1
documentation:
Check that an internal error is raised if the length of the Items
list is not 2 as the str() method assumes that it is.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_control_edit_descriptor_r1011.test_internal_error2
documentation:

(first entry of items) is empty or None as the str() method assumes
that it is a string with content.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_designator_r603
documentation:
Test Fortran 2003 rule R603 : This file tests support for the
Designator class.

Three of the rules in Designator class are currently not called as the
text matches to earlier rules. The associated rule constraints and
specification text (if implemented, which they are not - see issue
#201) would fix this problem.

For example, here is note 6.6 from the F2003 specification which helps
clarify for derived types:

The syntax rules are structured such that a data-ref that ends in a
component name without a following subscript list is a structure
component, even when other component names in the dataref are followed
by a subscript list. A data-ref that ends in a component name with a
following subscript list is either an array element or an array
section. A data-ref of nonzero rank that ends with a substring-range
is an array section. A data-ref of zero rank that ends with a
substring-range is a substring.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_designator_r603.test_array_element
documentation:
Test that the array-element rule is matched when the Designator
rule is supplied with an array-element.

# Note that at this time this example would also match rules
# array-section and structure-component (although it would give
# the same result so would not matter). It matches the expected
# rule (array-element) as this rule occurs before array-section
# and structure-component in the Designator class subclass_names
# list so is matched first.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_designator_r603.test_array_section
documentation:
Test that the array-section rule is matched when the Designator
rule is supplied with an array-section.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_designator_r603.test_array_section2
documentation:
Test that an Array_Section object is returned when a valid
array-section (followed by a substring-range) is supplied.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_designator_r603.test_structure_component
documentation:
Test that the structure-component rule is matched when the Designator
rule is supplied with a structure component.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_designator_r603.test_substring
documentation:
Test that an Substring object is returned when a valid substring is
supplied. This example provides the context (i.e. 'a' is a
character string) as otherwise it is not possible for the parser
to distinguish between a substring and an array section and it
defaults to the latter.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_specification_r1002.test_single
documentation:
Check that some basic format specifications with single format
items are parsed correctly. Individual items are tested by the
associated classes.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_specification_r1002.test_multi
documentation:
Check that a basic format specification with multiple format items
are parsed correctly. Individual format items are tested by the
associated classes.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_specification_r1002.test_spaces
documentation:
Check that a basic format specification with multiple format items
are parsed correctly with spaces. Individual format items are
tested by the associated classes. Note, the standard states that
spaces can occur anywhere within a format specifier and not affect
the meaning, other than within a format string. This is checked by
the individual format item classes.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_specification_r1002.test_hollerith
documentation:
Check that a basic format specification with a hollerith string
item is parsed correctly. Individual format items are tested by
the associated classes.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_specification_r1002.test_c1002
documentation:
Check that format specifications conforming to the C1002
constraints are parsed correctly. This test actually checks class
Format_Item_C1002 when a comma is missing as the constraints have
been implemented within this class in fparser.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_specification_r1002.test_c1002_clash
documentation:
The constraints rules can clash with each other. Here we test what
happens when they do. fparser assumes that we can break the P rule,
as does PGI. In contrast gfortran allows P / but does not allow P
: (which seems strange).

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_specification_r1002.test_c1002_triples
documentation:
Test that we get expected behaviour when the C1002 rule applies to
a triplet of items.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_specification_r1002.test_syntaxerror
documentation: Test that we get an exception for incorrect bracket syntax.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_format_specification_r1002.test_syntaxerror_c1002
documentation:
Test that we get an exception in situations where no comma is
supplied and the C1002 constraints for optional commas do not
apply.

modules.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701
documentation:
Test Fortran 2003 rule R701 : primary type.

Sub-rules:
    C701 (R701) The type-param-name shall be the name of a type parameter.
    C702 (R701) The designator shall not be a whole assumed-size array

Neither C701 nor C702 can be tested here, as they require context of the
type defined outside of Primary.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.assert_subclass_parse
documentation:
Assert that the given source matches the given `base_type`
and optionally the specific type that it should produce.

```
param source

The Fortran source to be parsed.

type source

str or 

param base_type

the base type from which a match is expected to be found

type base_type

 subclass

param actual_type

The actual type matched by the parser.

type actual_type

 subclass

param str expected_str

The expected str(result) of the parsed result
```

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.possible_subclasses
documentation:
Given a type (e.g. Fortran2003.Primary), return all of the
subtypes that could have been matched after parsing.

NOTE: This is not a general implementation. It is useful for testing
in some limited situations. Please refer to the Base.\_\_new\_\_ for the
actual logic for identifying possible_subclasses.

```
param node_type

The root node from which to find all subclasses.

type node_type

 subclass

param _seen

Private list of seen subclasses, designed to support                   recursive calls to this function.

type _seen

None or list
```

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_intrinsic_function
documentation: Test that an intrinsic function is matched by Primary.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_constant
documentation: Test that Constant types are matched by Primary.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_designator
documentation: Test that Designator types are matched by Primary.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_array_constructor
documentation: Test that Array Constructor types are matched by Primary.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_structure_constructor_1
documentation: Test that Structure Constructor types are matched by Primary.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_structure_constructor_2
documentation:
Test that Structure Constructor types are matched by Primary.

This test currently fails as PERSON( 12, 24 ) is matched as an
array access (Designator). In general, the only way to tell the
difference is to know whether PERSON is the name of a structure or
an array. However, we don't keep this information at the moment
and even when we do in the future, we still might
not know the information before link time as declarations may be
in different modules (see issue #201).

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_function_reference
documentation:
This test demonstrates the inability to distinguish
Structure_Constructor from Function_Reference without more parse context
than is currently being provided.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_type_param_inquiry
documentation:
This test demonstrates the inability to distinguish Designator from
Type_Param_Inquiry without more parse context than is currently being
provided.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_type_param_name
documentation: Test that Type_Param_Name types are matched by Primary.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_parenthesis
documentation:
Test that Parenthesis types are matched by the Primary. As
fparser2 implements this match as a separate class called
`Parenthesis`, also check this class directly.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_parenthesis_no_match
documentation:
Test that invalid Parenthesis input is not matched by Primary or
Parenthesis classes.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_no_match
documentation:
Test that a NoMatchError is raised if we provide code
that isn't allowed as a Primary type (e.g. a comment).

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_c701_no_assumed_size_array
documentation:
Test C701 (R701) The type-param-name shall be the name of a type.
This test cannot be passed without more parse context of things like
defined types.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.fortran2003.test_primary_r701.test_c702_no_assumed_size_array
documentation:
Test C702 (R701) The designator shall not be a whole assumed-size array.
This test cannot be passed without more parse context of things like
defined types.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_binaryopbase_pattern_nomatch
documentation:
Test the BinaryOpBase match method returns None if the pattern is
of type 'str' or 'Pattern' and is not found in the string. Check
with the optional 'right' argument set to True and False.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_binaryopbase_empty_nomatch
documentation:
Test the BinaryOpBase match method returns None if the lhs or the
rhs of the string is empty.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_binaryopbase_exclude_nomatch
documentation:
Test the BinaryOpBase match method returns None if the matching
pattern is excluded.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_binaryopbase_right_nomatch
documentation:
Test the BinaryOpBase match method checks whether classes match
using the rhs string first and the lhs string second if the
'right' optional argument is true and vice versa if the 'right'
optional argument is false.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_binaryopbase_match
documentation:
Test the BinaryOpBase match method returns the expected results if
there is a match for a pattern of type str and Pattern. Also check
that spaces do not affect matching and that they are removed in
the results.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_binaryopbase_addition
documentation:
Test that an addition involving a numerical constant with an
exponent containing '+' is parsed correctly.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_upper
documentation:
Test that the case of the matched pattern is unchanged if the match
is a string but it is made upper case if it is a Pattern. The
upper-casing does not seem to serve any purpose so should probably
be removed, see issue #282.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_spaces
documentation:
Tests that BinaryOpBase matches with spaces in a pattern but
removes the spaces in the output when there is a match. There
seems to be no reason to do this so it should probably be removed,
see issue #282.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_repmap
documentation:
Test that repmap is used correctly to remove appropriate patterns
that might falsely match and that the values are returned
appropriately if there is a match.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_binaryopbase.test_tostr
documentation:
Test that the BinaryOpBase tostr method returns the expected
output. It is not possible to instantiate BinaryOpBase directly as
the number of arguments expected in the constructor differs from
the number required in the match (so an error is
raised). Therefore we use an existing subclass - Level_4_Expr - to
test.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_sequencebase.test_match_invalid_string
documentation:
Test the sequencebase match method raises the expected exception if
arg string is not a string.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_sequencebase.test_match
documentation: Test the sequencebase match method with 1 or more entries.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_sequencebase.test_match_repmap
documentation:

is also used within the matching class.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_sequencebase.test_match_space
documentation:
Test the sequencebase match method raises an exception when the
separator is white space.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_sequencebase.test_match_instance
documentation: Test the sequencebase init, tostr and torepr methods.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_blockbase.test_include
documentation:
Test the BlockBase match method supports include statements and
comments before the class that indicates the start of the block
(the start class).
TODO #179: full testing of this class. We currently only test the
comment and include support.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_blockbase.test_strict_order_invalid_code
documentation:
Check that the strict_order flag toggles the parse behaviour as
expected.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_blockbase.test_strict_order_valid_code
documentation: Tests that the strict_order keyword allows repeated types.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_blockbase.test_label_do_construct_hook_match
documentation:
Check that specifying the enable_do_label_construct_hook to the match
method allows multiple loops sharing the same label to be matched.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_blockbase.test_label_do_nomatch
documentation:
Check that the match() method returns None for an invalid do
statement.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_blockbase.remove_indentation
documentation:
A utility function that removes indented multiline strings of reprs.

```
param str string

the string to dedent.

returns

a dedented version of the string.

rtype

str
```

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_blockbase.test_syntax_error_nested_symbol_table
documentation:
Test that a syntax error within a nested scoping region is handled
correctly. We use some code that has a mis-spelt attribute on a
declaration to trigger a syntax error.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_brackets
documentation:
Test the bracketsbase match method with no content within the
brackets.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_input_too_short
documentation:
Test the bracketsbase match method returns None when the input
string is shorter than the brackets.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_cls
documentation:
Test the bracketsbase match method with content within the brackets
for both require_cls is False and True. require_cls indicates
whether content should be expected within the brackets (and the
match failing if not), or whether it is optional. The actual
contents are passed on to the specified class ('Name' in this
case) to match (or not), hence the name require_cls.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_trailing_whitespace
documentation:
Test that the BracketBase match method passes any trailing whitespace
within the brackets to the specified class.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_brackets_error1
documentation:
Test the bracketbase match method returns None if the brackets are
invalid.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_brackets_error2
documentation:
Test the bracketbase match method returns the brackets if class contents
are expected but there are none.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_brackets_error3
documentation:
Test the bracketbase match method returns None if class contents
are expected (as the default is require_cls=True) but there are
none.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_tostr
documentation:
It is not possible to instantiate BracketBase directly so we create
a class that uses BracketBase (Format_Specification) and then test
the tostr() method from it. This test checks that an internal
error is raised if the size of the internal items list is
incorrect.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_tostr_invalid2
documentation:
It is not possible to instantiate BracketBase directly so we create
a class that uses BracketBase (Format_Specification) and then test
the tostr() method from it. This test checks that an internal
error is raised if entry 0 of the internal items list is empty or
None.

functions.name: fparser.two.tests.utils.test_bracket_base.test_tostr_invalid3
documentation:
It is not possible to instantiate BracketBase directly so we create
a class that uses BracketBase (Format_Specification) and then test
the tostr() method from it. This test checks that an internal
error is raised if entry 2 of the internal items list is empty or
None.
